dragonball.hero_subtitle: Every Saiyan transformation, character profile, technique guide, and saga breakdown — covering Dragon Ball, DBZ, GT, and Super.

characters.name: Son Goku
characters.role: Main Protagonist / Saiyan raised on Earth
characters.desc: Son Goku, born Kakarot, is the central protagonist of the Dragon Ball franchise and arguably the most iconic anime hero in history. Sent to Earth as an infant Saiyan with a mission to conquer the planet, a head injury erased his destructive programming and left him with a pure, cheerful heart. Raised by Grandpa Gohan in the wilderness, Goku grew up with an innate love for martial arts, food, and adventure. His journey began when a teenage Bulma accidentally hit him with her car, leading to their partnership in searching for the mystical Dragon Balls. Under the tutelage of Master Roshi, Korin, King Kai, and later Whis, Goku evolved from a naive boy with a tail into the multiverse's mightiest warrior. He was the first Saiyan in over a thousand years to achieve Super Saiyan, a legendary transformation triggered by Frieza's murder of Krillin on Planet Namek. Over the decades, Goku has mastered dozens of forms including Super Saiyan 2, Super Saiyan 3, Super Saiyan God, Super Saiyan Blue, and the divine Ultra Instinct technique that allows his body to move independently of thought. Despite his godlike power, Goku remains defined by his childlike innocence, insatiable appetite, unshakeable optimism, and relentless drive to fight stronger opponents. He has faced and befriended former enemies including Piccolo, Vegeta, Beerus, and even Frieza when circumstances demanded it. His signature Kamehameha wave, Spirit Bomb, and Instant Transmission have become cultural touchstones recognized worldwide. Goku's legacy extends beyond fiction: he is a symbol of self-improvement, boundless potential, and the idea that even the humblest beginnings can lead to extraordinary heights.
characters.power_level: Base: 10,000 (Saiyan Saga); Super Saiyan: 150,000,000 (Namek Saga); SSJ3: 4e14; Super Saiyan God: 7e15; SSB: 2e16; Ultra Instinct: beyond mortal measurement

characters.name: Vegeta
characters.desc: Vegeta, the proud Prince of all Saiyans, is Goku's greatest rival and the deuteragonist of the Dragon Ball series. Introduced as a ruthless planet conqueror under Frieza's command, Vegeta arrived on Earth seeking the Dragon Balls and immortality, only to suffer his first humiliating defeat at the hands of Goku and the Z-Fighters. That loss ignited an obsessive drive to surpass Goku that has defined Vegeta's entire arc across four decades of storytelling. Born royalty on Planet Vegeta as the son of King Vegeta III, he was taken as a child by Frieza and molded into one of the galaxy's deadliest warriors. His Saiyan pride is both his greatest strength and deepest flaw: it fuels his relentless training, yet has led him to catastrophic decisions, including allowing Cell to absorb Android 18 just to fight a stronger opponent. Vegeta's redemption arc is among the most celebrated in anime: from mass murderer to reluctant ally, to devoted husband of Bulma and protective father of Trunks and Bulla. During the Tournament of Power, he refused to abandon his family's future and unlocked Super Saiyan Blue Evolution. Under Beerus's guidance, he learned to harness the power of destruction and achieved the fearsome Ultra Ego form that grows stronger the more damage he absorbs. Unlike Goku, Vegeta fights with strategic precision: his Final Flash, Galick Gun, Big Bang Attack, and Final Explosion are delivered with calculated brutality. His mentorship of Cabba from Universe 6 revealed a newfound capacity for teaching the next generation. Vegeta's greatest victory was not any battle, but his internal conquest over his own demons, choosing family and honor over mindless ambition. He remains the ultimate testament that even the darkest past can be overcome through discipline, love, and an unbreakable will to improve.
characters.power_level: Saiyan Saga: 18,000; Namek Saga: 24,000 (post-Zarbon); Super Saiyan: 150,000,000; SSB Evolution: 2.5e16; Ultra Ego: scales with damage taken

characters.name: Gohan
characters.desc: Gohan, the eldest son of Goku and Chi-Chi, possesses the greatest latent potential of any warrior in the Dragon Ball universe. Named after his adoptive grandfather, Gohan was thrust into battle at the tender age of four when Raditz kidnapped him, triggering a rage-fueled headbutt that shocked even Piccolo. Recognizing the boy's dormant power, Piccolo took Gohan into the wilderness for a brutal year of survival training, forging a teacher-student bond that would become one of the series' most enduring relationships. Unlike his battle-obsessed father, Gohan is a gentle pacifist at heart, preferring academic pursuits and family life over combat. Yet when pushed beyond his limits, his explosive rage transforms him into a force that even gods respect. His defining moment came during the Cell Games at age eleven: after witnessing the death of Android 16 and the suffering of his friends, Gohan snapped and became the first to achieve Super Saiyan 2. His one-armed Father-Son Kamehameha, spiritually guided by Goku from the afterlife, obliterated Super Perfect Cell and saved the world. Years later, Old Kai unleashed his full potential through a ritual, creating the Ultimate Gohan, a state where all latent power surfaces without needing Super Saiyan. In Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero, rage over Piccolo's near-death awakened his Beast Form, a transformation with white hair and red eyes rivaling Ultra Instinct itself. Gohan is also a loving husband to Videl and devoted father to Pan. His dual identity as the Great Saiyaman, a goofy costumed hero, reflects his desire to protect without the burden of fame. Gohan represents a different kind of strength: the power that emerges when goodness and restraint are pushed past their breaking point.
characters.power_level: Base (latent/infant): 1; Rage Boost vs Raditz: 1,307; SSJ2 (Cell Games): 3.5e14; Ultimate Form: 1.2e17; Beast Form: beyond current measurement

characters.name: Piccolo
characters.desc: Piccolo Jr., known simply as Piccolo, is the reincarnation and son of the Demon King Piccolo who once terrorized Earth. Born from a single egg spat out by his dying father at the 23rd World Martial Arts Tournament, Piccolo entered the world with a single purpose: to kill Son Goku and avenge his father. He faced Goku in the tournament finals in a battle of unprecedented brutality, losing by a hair's breadth. Rather than dying, Piccolo's arc became a character study in radical evolution. Forced to ally with Goku against the Saiyan threat, he took Gohan as his student and discovered something he had never known: love. Training the young half-Saiyan awakened a paternal instinct that steadily eroded his demonic nature. By the time he sacrificed himself to save Gohan from Nappa's attack, Piccolo had fully transitioned from villain to protector. After fusing with the Namekian warrior Nail on Planet Namek, his power skyrocketed, allowing him to hold his own against second-form Frieza. His later fusion with Kami, his own other half, reunited him into the original Nameless Namekian, granting power and wisdom surpassing a Super Saiyan. As Gohan's lifelong mentor, Piccolo designed the boy's signature purple uniform, taught him to fight, and remained his most trusted guardian. In Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero, Shenron unlocked Piccolo's latent potential and granted him the Orange Piccolo transformation, a hulking orange-skinned form with power exceeding Super Saiyan Blue. A tactical genius with regenerative abilities, elastic limbs, and the devastating Special Beam Cannon, Piccolo remains the unshakeable backbone of the Z-Fighters, the calm strategist who anchors the team when Saiyan tempers flare.

characters.name: Frieza
characters.desc: Frieza stands as the most notorious villain in Dragon Ball history, a galactic tyrant whose cruelty shaped the fates of entire civilizations across Universe 7. A mutant prodigy of his unnamed species with power dwarfing even his father King Cold, Frieza built the Planet Trade Organization into a galaxy-spanning empire that conquered and sold worlds to the highest bidder. His most consequential act was the annihilation of Planet Vegeta, exterminating nearly the entire Saiyan race out of fear of the legendary Super Saiyan prophecy. When his forces clashed with Goku and the Z-Fighters on Planet Namek, Frieza revealed himself to be terrifyingly powerful: even at a fraction of his full strength, he outclassed the entire team. The battle that followed is a defining milestone in anime history. After Frieza murdered Krillin before Goku's eyes, the Saiyan's rage awakened the first Super Saiyan transformation in a millennium. Frieza was sliced in half by his own attack and left for dead, but his resilience is legendary. Resurrected by his loyal forces decades later, Frieza achieved the Golden Frieza form after just four months of training, a feat that took Goku and Vegeta years to match. His temporary alliance with Universe 7 in the Tournament of Power revealed a new dimension to the character: a cunning, pragmatic survivor who could work alongside his hated enemies when survival demanded it. In the Granolah arc, Frieza unveiled his ultimate evolution, Black Frieza, achieved after ten years of training in a Hyperbolic Time Chamber. With a single punch, he one-shotted both Ultra Instinct Goku and Ultra Ego Vegeta simultaneously, reasserting himself as the most dangerous being in the universe. Frieza's polite demeanor masking bottomless sadism makes him an enduring and uniquely chilling antagonist.

characters.name: Cell
characters.desc: Cell is Dr. Gero's ultimate creation, a bio-android genetically engineered from the cells of the greatest warriors to ever visit Earth, including Goku, Vegeta, Piccolo, Frieza, and even King Cold. Grown in Dr. Gero's subterranean laboratory and completed years after his creator's death, Cell emerged in an imperfect larval form that relied on absorbing human life energy to grow stronger. His true design required absorbing Androids 17 and 18 to reach his Perfect Form. The Imperfect Cell was a calculating predator stalking cities and absorbing victims through his needle-tipped tail, while Semi-Perfect Cell became a hulking, brutish monster after absorbing Android 17. When he finally absorbed Android 18 and achieved perfection, Cell transformed into a sleek, composed warrior with unprecedented power and arrogant confidence. He announced the Cell Games, a televised martial arts tournament where Earth's strongest would face him with the planet's fate at stake. The tournament culminated in one of the franchise's greatest moments: Gohan, pushed beyond his limits by Cell's cruelty, achieved Super Saiyan 2 and destroyed the bio-android with a one-armed Father-Son Kamehameha after Cell attempted to self-destruct and destroy Earth. Cell's genetic composition grants him access to nearly every signature technique in the series: Kamehameha, Special Beam Cannon, Destructo Disc, Instant Transmission, and even imperfect versions of the Spirit Bomb. He can regenerate from a single cell nucleus, spawn destructive Cell Juniors, and evolve through absorption. His blend of Frieza's cold sadism, Vegeta's pride, and Goku's love of battle created a uniquely compelling villain who challenged the heroes on every level.

characters.name: Majin Buu
characters.desc: Majin Buu is an ancient primordial force of destruction who has existed since time immemorial, cyclically awakened and sealed away by the warlock Bibidi. Unlike any other villain in the series, Buu manifests in multiple distinct personalities and forms, each with its own temperament and power level. The original Kid Buu was pure, mindless chaos, a pink-skinned creature of absolute destruction who destroyed countless galaxies before being sealed in a magical cocoon. When Bibidi's son Babidi awakened him on Earth millennia later, the accumulated positive influence of absorbed kind-hearted victims, particularly the gentle Grand Supreme Kai, had transformed him into the childlike, naive Fat Buu. This version befriended Mr. Satan and a small dog named Bee, forming the first genuine emotional connections of his existence and learning that killing was wrong. However, the evil within Buu manifested as a separate entity, the thin grey Evil Buu, who absorbed Fat Buu to become the menacing Super Buu. Super Buu then absorbed Gotenks, Piccolo, and Ultimate Gohan to achieve even greater power levels, becoming Buutenks and Buuhan. Ultimately, Vegeta's desperate gambit freed the absorbed fighters, reverting Buu back to his original Kid Buu form, the weakest in raw power but by far the most dangerous due to his complete lack of restraint. Kid Buu destroyed Earth on a whim, forcing Goku and Vegeta to battle him on the Sacred World of the Kais. After an epic struggle, Goku destroyed Kid Buu with a Spirit Bomb powered by the entire Earth's population. The good Fat Buu survived and became Mr. Satan's roommate and a beloved Z-Fighter ally. Kid Buu's soul was reincarnated as Uub, a pure-hearted human boy whom Goku would later train as his successor.

characters.name: Trunks
characters.desc: Trunks is the son of Vegeta and Bulma, uniquely existing in two timeline variants: the grim future warrior who defied fate and the present-day kid who grew up in peace. Future Trunks first appeared as a mysterious Super Saiyan teenager who effortlessly destroyed Frieza and King Cold upon their arrival on Earth. Hailing from a devastated timeline where Androids 17 and 18 had killed nearly all the Z-Fighters, he traveled back in time to warn of the coming threat and deliver the heart medicine that would save Goku's life. His tragic backstory, training under a one-armed Future Gohan who was eventually murdered by the androids, is one of the franchise's most emotionally devastating arcs. Future Trunks returned years later in Dragon Ball Super to face the genocidal Zamasu and Goku Black, achieving the unique Super Saiyan Rage form that allowed him to challenge even Fused Zamasu, albeit temporarily. His final act in that timeline was witnessing Zeno erase the entire corrupted reality, a cosmic tragedy that left him as one of the few survivors resettled in a new timeline. Present-day Kid Trunks is a far more carefree character: brash, spoiled by his mother Bulma's wealth, and the best friend and Fusion Dance partner of Goten. Their fused form, Gotenks, achieved Super Saiyan 3 at a combined age of just fifteen. Kid Trunks inherited his father's pride and competitive nature yet shows glimpses of his mother's sharp intelligence. The contrast between the battle-hardened Future Trunks and the playful Kid Trunks illustrates how circumstances shape character. Both versions carry Vegeta's Saiyan pride and Bulma's ingenuity, wielding the legendary Sword of Hope, a blade that can be charged with ki to devastating effect.

characters.name: Bulma
characters.desc: Bulma Briefs is the brilliant heiress of Capsule Corporation, the wealthiest technology company on Earth, and arguably the most important supporting character in the entire Dragon Ball franchise. A teenage Bulma set the entire story in motion when she developed the Dragon Radar, a handheld device capable of locating the mystical Dragon Balls, and set out on a journey that led to her fateful encounter with a wild-haired boy named Goku in the wilderness. Though she lacks combat ability, Bulma's genius-level intellect has saved the world more times than any ki blast. She invented the Dragon Radar that made the Dragon Balls accessible, reverse-engineered and built a fully functional time machine that enabled Future Trunks to save two timelines, constructed the Super Saiyan God ritual apparatus and coordinated the search for five pure-hearted Saiyans, created the Blutz Wave Generator that allowed Vegeta to access Great Ape power without a tail, and her constant technological support from space-capable ships to advanced medical equipment has been the logistical backbone of the Z-Fighters. Bulma is also defined by her tempestuous personality: vain, short-tempered, and prone to hitting people, yet fiercely loyal, courageous, and deeply caring. Her relationship with Vegeta evolved from convenience to genuine love, producing two extraordinary children, Trunks and Bulla. Their dynamic, Bulma's fiery temper matched against Vegeta's stubborn pride, provides the series with some of its most humorous and human moments. As Goku's oldest living friend, she maintains a bond with him rooted in their shared childhood adventures. Bulma represents the power of human intellect in a universe of gods, proving that brains can indeed match brawn in a world defined by ever-escalating power levels.

characters.name: Krillin
characters.desc: Krillin is Goku's oldest and most loyal friend and the strongest pure-human fighter in Universe 7. The two met as rival students under Master Roshi's Kame School, where Krillin initially schemed to sabotage Goku but quickly became his inseparable companion. Through decades of battles against planet-destroying threats, Krillin has demonstrated that courage is not measured by power level alone. Despite being vastly outclassed by Saiyans, androids, and gods, he has never retreated from a fight when his friends were in danger. His tactical brilliance gave the Z-Fighters crucial advantages: on Planet Namek, his Destructo Disc caught Frieza off guard and sliced off the tyrant's tail, an achievement no other fighter had managed. His death at Frieza's hands, gruesomely blown apart by telekinetic force, triggered Goku's legendary transformation into the first Super Saiyan. Krillin's most unexpected victory came not on a battlefield but in love: he married Android 18, a former enemy, and together they have a daughter named Marron. His affection for his family is his driving motivation, he fights to protect them, not for Saiyan pride. During the Tournament of Power, Krillin's tactical acumen shone as he devised combination attacks and used the Solar Flare x100 against far stronger opponents. Trained initially by Master Roshi and later by Kami and King Kai, Krillin's techniques emphasize precision over brute force. His Destructo Disc can bisect enemies many orders of magnitude stronger, and his Scattering Bullet provides area suppression. Though he retired from active combat to work as a police officer and raise his family, Krillin remains an essential Z-Fighter. He is living proof that the human spirit, armed with intelligence and loyalty, can stand alongside gods.

characters.name: Beerus
characters.desc: Lord Beerus is the God of Destruction of Universe 7, tasked with maintaining cosmic balance by destroying planets, civilizations, and even entire species when necessary. Despite his fearsome title, Beerus is more often found napping for decades at a time, indulging his obsession with Earth's cuisine, and delegating most responsibilities to his attendant and teacher, the Angel Whis. His awakening from a thirty-nine-year slumber set the events of Dragon Ball Super in motion: driven by a prophetic dream of a Super Saiyan God who would become his ultimate rival, he traveled to Earth and challenged Goku. Their battle, spectacular in scale with shockwaves rippling across the universe, ended with Goku absorbing the power of Super Saiyan God into his base form and earning Beerus's grudging respect. Though he spared Earth, Beerus has consistently served as the ultimate benchmark for Goku and Vegeta's growth. His power dwarfs nearly everything in the franchise: with a casual gesture, he knocked out Super Saiyan 3 Goku; with a sneeze, he can shatter planets. He has demonstrated an incomplete version of Ultra Instinct, and his Hakai technique erases targets from existence across all planes. As a character, Beerus is mercurial: one moment playful and food-obsessed, the next terrifyingly destructive. Despite his capricious nature, he has shown genuine investment in Goku and Vegeta's development, pushing them toward Ultra Instinct and Ultra Ego respectively. His life is linked to the Supreme Kai Shin: if Shin dies, Beerus dies, a vulnerability he must carefully guard. His rivalry with his twin brother Champa, the God of Destruction of Universe 6, produces some of the series' most comedic moments. Beerus embodies the theme that even gods are flawed, evolving beings rather than static omnipotent entities.

characters.name: Whis
characters.desc: Whis is the Angel attendant to Beerus and serves as both a cosmic guardian and Goku and Vegeta's most transformative martial arts instructor. As a member of the Angel Corps, divine beings who serve the twelve Gods of Destruction across the multiverse, Whis is part of the most powerful known race in existence, answerable only to the Grand Priest and Zeno himself. His true power level is incomprehensible: he has casually knocked out Beerus with a single chop and moves at speeds that appear to transcend time itself. Whis is the only known being to have fully mastered Ultra Instinct, a state where every cell in the body reacts autonomously, eliminating the delay between perception and response. Where Goku struggles to maintain Ultra Instinct for minutes, Whis operates in it perpetually as his natural state of being. Despite his near-omnipotence, he possesses a remarkably gentle, almost whimsical personality. His greatest obsessions are Earth's cuisine, which he samples with the seriousness of a Michelin inspector, and his personal grooming routine. His Staff of Authority grants him powerful abilities including instantaneous universal travel, retroactive time rewind of up to three minutes, and the projection of cosmic holograms. As a teacher, Whis's methods are unconventional: he emphasizes instinct over technique, relaxation over tension, and flow over force, often assigning absurd household chores as training exercises. The Angels are bound by a strict non-intervention law: they cannot fight or take sides in mortal conflicts, and if their God of Destruction dies, they become inactive until a replacement is appointed. Whis has proven to be a transformative presence in Dragon Ball Super, not only as a power ceiling but as the philosophical guide who introduced Goku to the principle that a calm mind produces a sharper fist.
characters.power_level: Angel tier: far surpasses Beerus. Estimated 5e20+. Supreme mastery of Ultra Instinct in permanent state.

characters.name: Broly
characters.desc: Broly is a mutant Saiyan of extraordinary birth, an anomaly whose power at infancy exceeded most elite Saiyan warriors. His existence as the Legendary Super Saiyan was prophesied in Saiyan folklore: a warrior of limitless growth who appears once every thousand years, his power ceaselessly expanding during battle without any apparent upper limit. In the canon film Dragon Ball Super: Broly, he was exiled as an infant by King Vegeta out of fear of his prodigious power level and banished to the desolate planet Vampa alongside his father Paragus. Stranded for over forty years, Broly grew up in extreme isolation with only his bitter, manipulative father for company, never learning social norms or proper combat technique, relying instead on raw instinct and uncontrollable rage. When rescued by Cheelai and Lemo of the Frieza Force and brought to Earth, Broly was unleashed as a weapon against Goku and Vegeta. The resulting battle was cataclysmic: despite having virtually no formal training, Broly's power adapted and escalated against every transformation thrown at him. Starting at a level comparable to base Vegeta, within hours he was overwhelming Super Saiyan Blue Goku and forcing the unprecedented fusion into Gogeta. His unique Wrathful State channels the power of the Great Ape without transforming, and his full Legendary Super Saiyan form with green-tinted hair makes him a walking cataclysm. Unlike his non-canon film counterpart, a sadistic vengeful monster, canon Broly is fundamentally innocent and gentle, a victim of his father's manipulation and his own uncontrollable power. After being defeated by Gogeta and rescued by Cheelai, Broly now trains on Beerus's planet alongside Goku and Vegeta, learning to harness his limitless potential peacefully.
characters.power_level: Base: ~1e14; Wrathful State: 1e15+; Legendary Super Saiyan: 2.5e16+ and growing limitlessly during battle

characters.name: Jiren
characters.desc: Jiren the Grey is the foremost warrior of Universe 11 and widely regarded as the strongest mortal to ever exist, a being whose raw power surpassed his own God of Destruction, Belmod, long before the Tournament of Power. Unlike Goku and Vegeta, who draw strength from bonds and emotion, Jiren embodies an opposing philosophy: absolute solitary strength, forged in the crucible of unimaginable loss. As a child, Jiren witnessed his entire family and martial arts master, Gicchin, murdered by an evil entity. When his fellow students abandoned him out of fear and self-preservation, Jiren internalized a devastating lesson: trust is weakness, and only through unshakeable individual power can one never be betrayed again. This trauma shaped him into a stoic, nearly emotionless colossus whose meditation-strengthened body can repel attacks before they land. His fighting style is brutally minimalist: overwhelming force applied with mechanical precision, eschewing complex techniques for raw, perfected fundamentals. During the Tournament of Power, Jiren remained virtually untouchable through the entire competition, one-shotting Kale in her Berserk form, effortlessly countering Hit's Time-Skip, and shrugging off a combined Spirit Bomb with a single glare. His power was such that even Ultra Instinct Sign Goku could barely match him. He was only defeated when Goku, Frieza, and Android 17, three former enemies, combined their efforts in a selfless display of teamwork, the very philosophy Jiren had rejected. The sight of their trust and cooperation, coupled with Toppo's heartfelt words, finally cracked his emotional armor. Jiren's character arc represents the franchise's central theme: that bonds do not weaken a warrior, they empower them beyond what any individual can achieve alone. His Overheat Magnum, Power Impact, and Infinity Rush attacks are devastating expressions of an existence dedicated entirely to strength.
characters.power_level: Surpassed his own God of Destruction (Belmod). Estimated 2e18+. Stronger than all Universe 11 deities.

characters.name: Tien Shinhan
characters.desc: Tien Shinhan is the stoic, disciplined three-eyed warrior descended from the ancient Triclops race and one of Earth's most accomplished martial artists. Introduced as an antagonist trained by the ruthless Crane Hermit Shen, Tien was initially Goku's fiercest rival in the 22nd World Martial Arts Tournament. Their finals match remains a masterclass in technical fighting choreography, showcasing Tien's mastery of the Multi-Form Technique, Four Witches Technique, Solar Flare, and the devastating Tri-Beam. Master Roshi's wisdom during the tournament broke through Tien's brainwashing, revealing to him the moral emptiness of the Crane School's philosophy. He abandoned Shen, joined the Turtle Hermit's path of honorable combat, and became a steadfast Z-Fighter. Tien's most legendary moment came during the Cell Saga: armed only with his Neo Tri-Beam, a technique that drains the user's life force with each use, he repeatedly blasted Semi-Perfect Cell into a crater, buying precious time for Android 18 to escape despite knowing each attack brought him closer to death. His companion Chiaotzu has been at his side since childhood, and their bond is the series' most affecting relationship. Tien lives a simple life running the Tien-Shin Dojo in the mountains with Chiaotzu, occasionally visited by Launch. He consistently trains harder than any other human fighter, maintaining relevance against ever-escalating threats through sheer discipline. Though outclassed by Saiyans and gods, his Tri-Beam, Solar Flare, and Multi-Form techniques remain tactically invaluable. Tien represents the martial arts ethos at its purest: relentless self-improvement, unwavering loyalty to friends, and a willingness to sacrifice everything when the moment demands it.
characters.power_level: Saiyan Saga: 1,830; Cell Saga: ~5,000,000; Buu Saga: ~500,000,000; Tournament of Power: tactical-level fighter

characters.name: Yamcha
characters.desc: Yamcha is one of Goku's oldest companions and represents the classic martial artist whose courage far outpaces his relative power. Introduced as a sixteen-year-old desert bandit armed with a sword and his signature Wolf Fang Fist, Yamcha's initial encounter with Goku, Bulma, and Oolong ended with his signature comedic weakness, a paralyzing fear of women that rendered him helpless around Bulma. Overcoming this phobia, he joined the Dragon Team and became a dedicated student of Master Roshi, competing in the 21st and 22nd World Martial Arts Tournaments. His Wolf Fang Fist, a rapid barrage of claw-like hand strikes delivered with feral intensity, and the Spirit Ball, a controllable floating ki sphere, established his reputation as one of Earth's most creative martial artists. Yamcha's most infamous moment, often comically exaggerated in fan culture, came during the Saiyan Saga when a Saibaman latched onto him and self-destructed, killing him. Yet Yamcha's bravery in that moment, he had volunteered for the dangerous first round and already defeated the Saibaman before the suicide attack, is often overlooked. After being revived, Yamcha's role shifted to support as the power scaling of the series left human fighters behind. He found a second career as a professional baseball player for the Taitans, where his martial arts skills made him a star athlete. His long-term relationship with Bulma ended due to lingering infidelity issues, though the two remain friends. Despite his diminished combat role in later arcs, Yamcha's presence from the earliest chapters and his unwavering willingness to fight alongside his friends, even knowing he is hopelessly outmatched, demonstrate a different kind of heroism. He represents the everyday person who stands up to impossible odds, not because he can win, but because he refuses to run.

characters.name: Master Roshi
characters.desc: Master Roshi, also known as the Turtle Hermit or Muten Roshi, is the legendary martial arts master who trained Goku, Krillin, and generations of Earth's greatest fighters. Despite his perverted old man demeanor and comedic antics, Roshi is a figure of immense historical significance: he invented the Kamehameha wave, the most iconic energy technique in anime history, and was once the strongest martial artist on Earth, known as the God of Martial Arts. His training methods are deceptively simple, involving manual labor like milk delivery, farming, and swimming, which build foundational strength and discipline. Under his tutelage, Goku and Krillin transformed from talented novices into tournament-level warriors in just eight months. Roshi's most profound contribution to the series' philosophy came when he entered the 21st World Martial Arts Tournament disguised as Jackie Chun to defeat his own students, wanting them to experience loss and understand there would always be someone stronger, a lesson that drove both Goku and Krillin to train harder for the rest of their lives. His creation of the Evil Containment Wave, the Mafuba, defeated Demon King Piccolo at the cost of his own life, though he was later revived. During the Tournament of Power, the ancient master shocked the multiverse by demonstrating a zen-like state of combat mastery that allowed him to dodge attacks from Jiren, a feat that should have been impossible for a fighter with a power level of merely 139 by early Dragon Ball standards. His secret: three centuries of accumulated experience channeled into perfect timing and minimal movement. Roshi also taught the concept of moving well, learning well, playing well, eating well, and resting well, the balanced philosophy inherited from his own master Mutaito that remains the Turtle School's guiding principle. He is the moral anchor of the franchise's martial arts philosophy.
characters.power_level: Early DB: 139; Max Power Form: ~200 (DB); Tournament of Power (zen state): ~1e10 (skill-amplified)

characters.name: Android 18
characters.desc: Android 18, originally a human girl named Lazuli before being kidnapped and experimented on by Dr. Gero, is a remarkably nuanced character in Dragon Ball: a former villain turned loving wife and mother without losing her edge as a fierce combatant. Along with her twin brother Android 17, she was forcibly remodeled at the cellular level by Gero's biomechanical engineering and equipped with an infinite energy reactor that grants her unlimited stamina. Unlike the purely mechanical Androids 19 and 20, 18 is a cybernetically enhanced human who retained her free will and emotions, which ultimately led her to rebel against Gero's programming. During the Android Saga, 18 terrorized the Z-Fighters alongside her brother, effortlessly defeating Vegeta in their first encounter and humiliating the proud Saiyan prince. She later became the unwilling absorption target for Cell, an experience that nearly cost her life. After being rescued by Krillin's mercy, he destroyed the remote control that could have deactivated her, choosing love over strategy, 18's cold exterior gradually melted. Their unlikely romance became one of the series' most enduring relationships, producing a daughter named Marron. As a fighter, Android 18 combines infinite energy with cold precision and tremendous power: during the Tournament of Power, she fought on par with Super Saiyan-level opponents and formed a devastating tag-team with her brother 17, using their Accel Dance combination attack. Beyond combat, 18's sharp wit and deadpan humor provide a refreshing contrast to the earnest intensity of the Saiyan-dominated cast. Her character arc, from weapon of destruction to devoted mother who still enjoys breaking ribs in tournaments, illustrates that redemption does not require abandoning one's strength or personality.
characters.power_level: Android Saga: comparable to SSJ Vegeta (~150,000,000); Tournament of Power: SSB-tier through combat skill and infinite energy

characters.name: Goten
characters.desc: Goten is the youngest son of Goku and Chi-Chi, born shortly after the Cell Games and inheriting his father's cheerful personality, wild black hair, and prodigious Saiyan potential. He made history within the Dragon Ball universe as the youngest Super Saiyan ever, achieving the legendary transformation at the unprecedented age of seven, dwarfing even Gohan's record. Unlike his elder brother who had his potential unlocked through rage and trauma, Goten awakened his power casually during training with Chi-Chi, a testament to the extraordinary hybrid vigor of second-generation Saiyan-human offspring raised during peacetime. His unshakeable bond with Trunks, the son of Vegeta and Bulma, defines Goten's character arc: the two boys are inseparable, and their mastery of the Metamoran Fusion Dance produces Gotenks, a fused warrior of staggering power and unparalleled arrogance. Gotenks achieved Super Saiyan 3 at a combined age of just fifteen, a transformation that took Goku years of afterlife training to discover, and battled Super Buu with an arsenal of preposterously named techniques including the Super Ghost Kamikaze Attack and Galactic Donut. Goten himself is gentle-natured and somewhat naive, lacking the battle-honed edge of his father or brother. Chi-Chi prioritized his education, though Goten shows far more enthusiasm for play-fighting and superhero antics than academics. During the Tournament of Power, Goten and Trunks were left behind to guard Android 17's island, a decision that frustrated fans eager to see the young half-Saiyans in action. In Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero, the two boys had grown into teenagers, and their fusion as Gotenks, albeit imperfect, helped against Cell Max. Goten represents the peaceful future the Z-Fighters fought for: a Saiyan hybrid who can achieve Super Saiyan without tragedy, born into a world where his power exists to protect rather than avenge.

characters.name: Chi-Chi
characters.desc: Chi-Chi is the daughter of the Ox-King and the devoted wife of Son Goku. Far from being merely a nagging housewife as often misinterpreted, Chi-Chi was herself a formidable martial artist in her youth. Trained by her father in the Ox-King style, she competed in the 23rd World Martial Arts Tournament where she confronted the grown-up Goku, who had completely forgotten who she was, and demanded he honor their childhood marriage promise. Their tournament bout was a revelation: Chi-Chi's Ox-King style fighting, combined with her retractable blade helmet and razor-edged fan, genuinely pressured Goku. After their marriage, Chi-Chi dedicated her life to raising Gohan and later Goten, fiercely prioritizing their education over combat training. Her insistence that Gohan study rather than fight has drawn criticism from fans, yet Chi-Chi's perspective is utterly rational: she lost her husband for years at a time to death and training, watched her four-year-old son kidnapped and brutalized by aliens, and simply wanted her children to have a normal, safe life. Under the fierce domestic exterior lies deep love and terror: the terror of a mother whose family is constantly targeted by gods and monsters. Her strictness ensured Gohan became a respected scholar, while her secret training with Goten produced the youngest Super Saiyan in history. Chi-Chi also runs the Son family household with an iron fist, managing Goku's mountain-sized appetite through constant cooking. Despite her frustrations with Goku's obliviousness to money and social norms, their marriage endures through mutual, if unconventional, devotion. During the Tournament of Power, she permitted Goku to participate only after extracting a promise for his post-tournament earnings. Chi-Chi embodies a grounded, human counterbalance to the cosmic absurdity of the Saiyan world, a woman who fights not with ki but with the terrifying power of maternal love and domestic authority.

characters.name: Android 17
characters.role: Cyborg Ranger / Tournament of Power MVP
characters.desc: Android 17, originally a human boy named Lapis before being kidnapped and cybernetically enhanced by Dr. Gero alongside his twin sister Android 18, underwent the franchise's most stunning character metamorphosis. Introduced as a remorseless killer who gleefully murdered Dr. Gero the moment he was activated, 17 spent the Android Saga as a terrifying antagonist who matched Super Saiyan-level power with cold disinterest, treating the life-and-death battles as a game. After Cell absorbed him to reach his Semi-Perfect form and was later killed, 17 was revived by the Dragon Balls and chose an entirely different path. He left human civilization behind, becoming a solitary ranger protecting a remote island wildlife sanctuary from poachers, finding peace in nature and the company of animals. His redemption arc peaked spectacularly during the Tournament of Power, where he emerged as the unexpected MVP of the entire competition. Fighting with tactical brilliance and limitless stamina from his infinite energy reactor, 17 outlasted warriors far more powerful than himself through strategy, selflessness, and an unbreakable will. His final wish on the Super Dragon Balls, to restore all the erased universes rather than anything for himself, revealed the depth of his moral transformation and earned him universal admiration. Reunited with his sister 18 during the tournament, the twins demonstrated their devastating Accel Dance combination and a bond that survived decades of separation. In Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero, 17 made a brief but impactful appearance, still dedicated to his island sanctuary. His character arc from nihilistic killing machine to selfless hero who saved the entire multiverse through a single compassionate wish stands as one of the franchise's finest redemption narratives, proving that even a weapon designed for destruction can choose a life of quiet, profound purpose.

characters.name: Kid Buu
characters.desc: Kid Buu is the original and purest form of Majin Buu, stripped of all absorbed influences and reduced to the primal essence of destruction that has terrorized the universe since time immemorial. Unlike the other Buu forms that retain fragments of personality from their absorbed victims, Kid Buu is an entity of pure, mindless chaos: he does not negotiate, does not strategize, and does not even speak beyond guttural shrieks and childlike giggles as he destroys everything in his path. His very first act upon reverting to this form was to casually obliterate Earth with a single energy sphere, killing billions without a second thought. Kid Buu's power level is technically the lowest among Super Buu's evolved forms, yet he is universally acknowledged as the most dangerous iteration of the creature because he possesses absolutely no restraint and can regenerate from any damage, even from smoke particles. His fighting style is completely unpredictable, characterized by erratic movements, contorted body positions, and attacks that defy the conventions of martial arts. After destroying Earth, Kid Buu traveled to the Sacred World of the Kais where Goku and Vegeta made their desperate last stand. The battle tested both Saiyans to their absolute limits: Vegeta was beaten to near-death, Goku exhausted every ounce of energy as Super Saiyan 3, and even the fusion of the Kais failed to contain him. Ultimately, Goku destroyed Kid Buu with the largest Spirit Bomb ever created, powered by the willing energy of every human on Earth and the newly resurrected Namekian Dragon Balls' wish for Goku's stamina to be restored. In a rare act of cosmic mercy, Goku wished for Kid Buu to be reincarnated as a good person, leading to the birth of Uub, the pure-hearted human boy who would become Goku's final student. Kid Buu represents destruction in its most terrifying form: not evil with a purpose, but annihilation as instinct.
characters.power_level: 2.8e15. Weaker than Buuhan in raw power, but far more dangerous due to complete lack of restraint, infinite regeneration, and pure destructive instinct.

characters.name: Videl
characters.desc: Videl is the daughter of Mr. Satan, the wealthy and beloved World Martial Arts Champion, and grows from a brash, fame-seeking teenager into a courageous fighter and devoted wife. Initially introduced during the Buu Saga as a classmate of Gohan at Orange Star High School, Videl quickly deduced Gohan's identity as the Great Saiyaman and blackmailed him into teaching her to fly using ki. Her extraordinary natural talent allowed her to master flight in mere days, a feat that took the Z-Fighters years of training. Videl's tenacity and refusal to back down, even when brutally beaten by the monstrous Spopovich during the 25th World Martial Arts Tournament, earned Gohan's deep respect and ultimately his heart. Despite lacking Saiyan blood or supernatural abilities, Videl trained enough to participate in the Tournament alongside the Z-Fighters. After marrying Gohan, she settled into family life and gave birth to Pan, whom she named after the bread Gohan was eating when the baby kicked for the first time. While her combat role diminished after becoming a mother, Videl remains fiercely protective of her family. She played a crucial supportive role during the events of Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero, coordinating with Bulma and aiding in the defense against the Red Ribbon Army's resurgence. Her relationship with her father Mr. Satan is complex: she loves him but is frequently embarrassed by his bombastic public persona. Videl's arc from spoiled celebrity daughter to brave warrior and loving mother demonstrates that heroism isn't determined by genetics or power level, but by the courage to stand up for what matters, even when outmatched.

characters.name: Pan
characters.desc: Pan is the beloved granddaughter of Son Goku and the daughter of Gohan and Videl, representing the future generation of the Dragon Ball universe. As a quarter-Saiyan, she possesses extraordinary innate talent: by the age of three, she was already capable of flight and displayed combat instincts that astonished both her grandfather and Piccolo. In Dragon Ball GT, Pan took a central role as Goku's companion in the search for the Black Star Dragon Balls across the universe, serving as the heart of the adventure. Despite being frequently sidelined in major battles due to her young age, Pan's courage and determination consistently shine through. She pilots a spaceship at age ten, stands her ground against terrifying villains, and maintains her grandfather's adventurous spirit even when the odds are overwhelming. Her fighting style blends Videl's street-smart tenacity with flashes of her Saiyan heritage: moments of instinctive combat prowess that hint at Gohan's latent potential flowing through her veins. In Dragon Ball Super: Super Hero, a three-year-old Pan demonstrated remarkable ki control by flying around Capsule Corp and instinctively recognizing her father's energy during a crisis. Her relationship with Piccolo is particularly heartwarming, as the Namekian who once trained her father has become her devoted honorary grandfather, teaching her basic combat skills and watching over her with gentle pride. Pan represents the pure, hopeful future the Z-Fighters fought for across decades: a generation that inherits power not through trauma or galactic war, but through love and peaceful guidance.

characters.name: Uub
characters.desc: Uub is the pure-hearted human reincarnation of Kid Buu, born from Goku's dying wish that the primordial evil be reborn as a good person so they could fight again one day. Hailing from a poverty-stricken village in the tropics, Uub grew up unaware of his cosmic origin, knowing only that he possessed inexplicable strength and a gentle, timid nature. He first appeared at the 28th World Martial Arts Tournament as a ten-year-old boy competing to win prize money for his struggling village. When matched against Goku in the first round, Uub's untrained rage at Goku's taunts unleashed flashes of Kid Buu's terrifying power, enough to genuinely impress the Saiyan warrior. Recognizing the boy's potential, Goku abandoned the tournament mid-match and flew away with Uub on his back, declaring him his final student. The two settled in Uub's village where Goku trained him to harness his inherited power for good. In Dragon Ball GT, Uub played a significant role as a full-fledged Z-Fighter, fusing with the good Fat Buu to become Majuub, a warrior combining Kid Buu's raw power with Fat Buu's compassionate heart. Majuub fought valiantly against Baby Vegeta and Omega Shenron, holding his own against foes that even Super Saiyan 4 Goku struggled against. While GT's canonicity is debated, Uub's character represents a profound thematic bookend: the cycle of violence broken by Goku's compassion, transforming the universe's most destructive force into its newest protector. The mentor-student relationship between Goku and Uub mirrors Roshi's training of the young Goku, closing a generational loop of martial arts tradition.

characters.name: Future Trunks
characters.role: Time Traveler / Last Hope of the Future
characters.desc: Future Trunks is the battle-hardened survivor of an apocalyptic timeline whose journey through time altered the fate of two realities. Born in a future where Androids 17 and 18 had murdered nearly all the Z-Fighters, Trunks grew up in a world of ash and despair. His mentor and only remaining ally was Future Gohan, a one-armed warrior who trained the boy until the androids killed him as well. This trauma awakened Trunks's Super Saiyan transformation, but even that was not enough against the twin killers. Desperate, he used his mother Bulma's experimental time machine to travel back twenty years, warn the present-day Z-Fighters of the coming android threat, and deliver the heart medicine that would save Goku from dying of a viral disease. His first arrival — effortlessly destroying Frieza and King Cold with his sword — remains an entrance etched into anime legend. Trunks's second return to the past during the Cell Saga saw him training in the Hyperbolic Time Chamber and achieving Super Saiyan Grade 3, though its speed limitations proved his undoing against Cell. After Cell killed him with a single Death Beam through the chest, Trunks was revived and returned to his own timeline, where he permanently destroyed both Androids and the Imperfect Cell lurking there, finally bringing peace to his world. Years later in Dragon Ball Super, a resurrected Trunks faced an even greater nightmare: the genocidal Goku Black and immortal Zamasu, who murdered his mother Bulma and wiped out humanity. Achieving the unique Super Saiyan Rage form, he fought alongside Goku and Vegeta before witnessing the ultimate tragedy of Zeno erasing his entire timeline. Resettled in a new future where duplicates of himself and Mai already existed, Trunks carries the weight of having lost everything multiple times yet never surrendering to despair. His story is the franchise's most poignant meditation on sacrifice, loss, and the refusal to give up hope even when the universe itself seems determined to crush it.
characters.power_level: Super Saiyan debut: ~150,000,000; SSJ Grade 3: ~3e14; SSJ2 (DBS): 8e15; Super Saiyan Rage: 1.5e16+

characters.name: Chiaotzu
characters.desc: Chiaotzu is the diminutive, eternally childlike companion of Tien Shinhan, a skilled martial artist whose psychic abilities set him apart from the purely physical fighters of the Dragon Ball universe. Trained alongside Tien under the cruel Crane Hermit Shen, Chiaotzu possesses telekinesis powerful enough to immobilize opponents, levitate objects, and even manipulate internal organs. His most devastating technique is the self-destruct attack, which he used in a heartbreaking attempt to kill the Saiyan Nappa during the Saiyan Saga, wrapping his body around the giant warrior and detonating his own life force in a massive explosion. Though Nappa survived due to the Saiyan's immense durability, Chiaotzu's sacrifice remains one of the series' most emotionally resonant moments, especially for his partner Tien, who was powerless to stop him. Chiaotzu was later revived by the Dragon Balls and continued to train and live alongside Tien at the Tien-Shin Dojo. His childlike appearance and love of playing video games and watching television belie a warrior with decades of combat experience who faced the Demon King Piccolo's offspring, the Saiyan invasion, and countless other threats. Though his power level never reached the cosmic heights of the Saiyans, Chiaotzu's unique psychic arsenal, including the Dodon Ray and Telekinesis, provide tactical options that pure ki blasts cannot replicate. His unwavering loyalty to Tien and his simple, gentle nature make him a beloved if underutilized presence in the Z-Fighter ranks.

characters.name: Future Gohan
characters.desc: Future Gohan is the version of Gohan from the same ravaged timeline as Future Trunks, and his story is one of Dragon Ball's most profound tragedies. After the deaths of Goku from a heart virus and Vegeta, Piccolo, Krillin, and the other Z-Fighters at the hands of Androids 17 and 18, the teenage Gohan was left as Earth's sole defender. Without the Hyperbolic Time Chamber or any surviving mentors, he fought a hopeless guerrilla war against the twin androids for over a decade, suffering countless injuries and losing his left arm in battle. During those dark years, Gohan trained a young Trunks, the son of the fallen Vegeta and Bulma, becoming to the boy what Piccolo had been to him: a surrogate father, a mentor, and the last flicker of hope in a dying world. Their relationship, built on shared loss and desperate perseverance, is the emotional core of the History of Trunks television special, widely regarded as one of the franchise's finest storytelling achievements. In their final battle, Gohan faced both androids alone to buy Trunks time to escape, insisting the boy was Earth's last hope. The androids killed him mercilessly, his body falling in a rain-swept street as Trunks watched helplessly from afar. That moment of ultimate despair triggered Trunks's first Super Saiyan transformation. Gohan's legacy lived on through his student, who went on to defeat the androids and save two timelines. Future Gohan represents the raw essence of heroism: fighting not because victory is possible, but because surrendering would betray everything worth protecting. His version of the character shows what Gohan's boundless potential looks like when tempered purely by duty, loss, and love, without his father's guidance or the safety net of the Dragon Balls.

characters.name: Mr. Satan
characters.role: World Martial Arts Champion / False Hero
characters.desc: Mr. Satan, born Mark, is the flamboyant and bombastic World Martial Arts Champion who serves as one of Dragon Ball's most surprisingly important characters. A genuine martial arts champion by normal human standards, Mr. Satan won the 24th World Martial Arts Tournament fairly and has maintained his title through legitimate skill, albeit skill that is utterly insignificant compared to the cosmic powers of the Z-Fighters. However, it is precisely this gap that makes Mr. Satan's role so crucial: he is the bridge between the supernatural world of ki-wielding warriors and ordinary humanity. When the entire planet was needed to contribute energy for the Spirit Bomb that destroyed Kid Buu, it was Mr. Satan's voice, the one person every human trusted, that convinced billions to raise their hands. Without his fame and charisma, Goku would never have gathered enough energy. His relationship with Fat Buu is one of the franchise's most endearing: after finding the pink creature alone and confused, Mr. Satan befriended him, taught him basic morality, and ultimately reformed the being that had previously murdered most of humanity. When evil gunmen shot Mr. Satan, Buu's rage split him into good and evil halves, setting the Buu Saga's climax into motion. Mr. Satan is also the father of Videl and grandfather of Pan, whose love for him is genuine despite her exasperation at his public persona. Though The narcissistic publicity hound exterior is largely an act, underneath lies a genuinely brave man who, despite knowing he is hopelessly outmatched, will still step between danger and innocents when no one else can. His best moments come not from winning fights, but from inspiring ordinary people to believe in themselves, the ultimate heroism of the everyman in a world of gods.
characters.power_level: ~60 (peak human martial artist standard). Skilled legitimate fighter among normal humans. Game-changer through charisma and influence rather than ki.

characters.name: Yajirobe
characters.desc: Yajirobe is the gruff, food-obsessed ronin who exists as one of Dragon Ball's most underrated heroes despite his complete lack of interest in martial arts glory. Discovered by Goku during the Demon King Piccolo Saga, Yajirobe was a wandering swordsman who had survived by his wits and his blade in the wilderness. Despite his rough, unmotivated exterior, he demonstrated remarkable combat ability: after Goku defeated and mortally wounded King Piccolo, it was Yajirobe who climbed Korin Tower with Goku on his back and later, in a moment of pure cowardice-turned-courage, sliced through the demon king's offspring Cymbal with a single sword stroke. His most significant contribution to the fate of the universe came during the Saiyan Saga. As Vegeta transformed into a Great Ape and threatened to annihilate everyone, a terrified Yajirobe, hiding behind a rock, seized the critical moment to slice off Vegeta's tail with his katana, reverting the Saiyan prince to his base form and saving Goku, Krillin, and Gohan from certain death. Without Yajirobe's single sword stroke, no one would have survived to fight another day, and all of Dragon Ball history would have ended right there. Yajirobe later settled on Korin Tower, becoming the hermit cat's companion and the guardian of the Senzu Bean supply. His role as bean delivery service to the Z-Fighters, while comedic, is logistically essential: without Senzu Beans, many battles would have been lost. Yajirobe refuses to train or fight seriously, preferring to eat, nap, and complain, yet when the stakes are highest, he has proven capable of decisive, universe-saving action that no other character could have delivered. He is the ultimate reluctant hero, proof that even a lazy, cowardly, grumpy swordsman can change history when the moment demands it.

characters.name: Korin
characters.desc: Korin the immortal cat sage is among the oldest and wisest beings on Earth, having lived for over eight hundred years as the guardian of Korin Tower, a sacred pillar that connects the land below to Kami's Lookout high above. Despite his diminutive feline appearance, Korin is a martial arts master of extraordinary wisdom and skill, having trained Master Roshi centuries ago and later guiding Goku through his first major power leap. When Goku climbed the tower after being defeated by Mercenary Tao, Korin spent three days evading the boy's every attempt to seize the Sacred Water from him. The water itself was ordinary tap water; the true power came from the training, which sharpened Goku's reflexes to a superhuman edge and taught him to sense ki movement rather than rely on his eyes. Korin is also the sole cultivator of the mystical Senzu Beans, the reality-breaking legumes that instantly heal all wounds and restore full stamina with a single bite. The existence of Senzu Beans has saved the Z-Fighters countless times, turning certain defeat into possible victory. Korin continues to grow these beans on his tower, though his crops are frequently depleted by Yajirobe's gluttony and the Z-Fighters' ceaseless demand. His relationship with Kami and Mr. Popo establishes a chain of guardianship that has protected Earth for millennia. In Dragon Ball Super, Korin remains a source of wisdom and beans, occasionally visited by Goku for advice. His timeless, sleepy-cat demeanor and cryptic counsel mask an ancient intelligence that has witnessed hundreds of years of martial arts history. Korin embodies the quiet, patient wisdom that supports heroes without seeking glory, growing beans on a sacred tower while civilizations rise and fall below.

characters.name: Shenron
characters.desc: Shenron is the Eternal Dragon summoned by gathering all seven Earth Dragon Balls, a colossal serpentine entity of emerald scales and crimson eyes who emerges from the Dragon Balls in a pillar of golden light that darkens the sky. Created by Kami from the original Namekian Dragon Ball template, Shenron possesses the power to grant any single wish within the limits of his creator's power: he can resurrect the dead (though not the same person twice, and not those who died of natural causes), restore destroyed planets, unlock latent potential, and teleport beings across the cosmos. His deep, resonant voice and majestic presence command awe, yet his personality is surprisingly humble, addressing summoners with formal politeness and never displaying resentment even when called upon frivolously. Shenron's limitations are well-established: he cannot kill beings stronger than his creator, cannot grant wishes that exceed Kami's (or later Dende's) power, and cannot resurrect large groups en masse until Dende upgraded the Dragon Balls to grant two wishes. His summoning requires the speaker to recite a Namekian incantation, and the Dragon Balls scatter across the globe after each use, becoming inert stone for one year. Throughout the series, the Dragon Balls and Shenron have been used for everything from wishing for panties (Oolong's infamous request) to resurrecting every victim of Frieza and his forces across the universe. Shenron's role in Dragon Ball Super expanded significantly: Piccolo used a wish to unlock his Orange transformation, and the revelation of the Dragon Balls' connection to the Namekian race deepened their mythical significance. Shenron is not a character in the traditional sense, but rather a narrative device made majestic, the physical manifestation of hope for a universe where death is rarely permanent.

characters.name: Raditz
characters.desc: Raditz is the biological older brother of Goku (Kakarot) and the first Saiyan antagonist introduced in Dragon Ball Z, whose arrival on Earth fundamentally changed the trajectory of the entire franchise. A mid-class Saiyan warrior who served under Vegeta in the Frieza Force's Planet Trade Organization, Raditz traveled to Earth seeking his younger brother, expecting to find a fellow planet conqueror who had completed his mission of exterminating Earth's population. Instead, he found Goku, who had no memory of his Saiyan heritage and had become Earth's greatest protector. Raditz's revelation of Goku's alien origins, complete with the existence of Planet Vegeta, the Saiyan race, and the greater galactic empire they served, expanded the Dragon Ball universe from an Earth-bound martial arts story into a cosmic saga. In battle, Raditz proved overwhelmingly superior to both Goku and Piccolo, the two strongest fighters on Earth at the time, forcing them into an unprecedented alliance. The desperate fight ended with Goku sacrificing his own life to hold Raditz in place while Piccolo's Special Beam Cannon pierced through both brothers simultaneously. Raditz's legacy extends far beyond his brief appearance: his arrival introduced the concepts of power levels, alien races, space pods, scouters, and the Saiyan class system that would define Dragon Ball Z's entire lore. His death also triggered Vegeta's interest in Earth and the Dragon Balls, setting the Saiyan and Frieza Sagas in motion. Though short-lived as a combatant, Raditz carries narrative weight far exceeding his screen time, the catalyst who transformed a mystical martial arts story into an intergalactic epic.
characters.power_level: 1,200-1,500. Mid-class Saiyan. Outclassed by Goku and Piccolo individually but superior in their first encounter due to combat experience.

characters.name: Nappa
characters.desc: Nappa is the towering, bald Saiyan elite who served as Vegeta's second-in-command during the Saiyan invasion of Earth. A military general from Planet Vegeta's royal army, Nappa survived Frieza's destruction of the Saiyan homeworld because he was off-world with Vegeta and Raditz on a mission. A battle-hardened veteran of countless planetary conquests, Nappa wielded raw power that dwarfed anything the Z-Fighters had encountered. Upon arriving on Earth with Vegeta, he single-handedly demolished the assembled Z-Fighter team, killing Chiaotzu (self-destruct), Tien (in rage), and Piccolo (protecting Gohan) while shrugging off their strongest attacks with casual sadism. His most devastating technique, the Volcano Explosion, raised two fingers and instantaneously obliterated an entire city skyline. Nappa's defeat came at the hands of a newly returned Goku, who had completed King Kai's training and whose power level of over 8,000 utterly overwhelmed the Saiyan general. Vegeta, disgusted by Nappa's failure and injury, executed him without hesitation, casually tossing the broken warrior into the air and destroying him with a single energy blast. Nappa's pragmatic cruelty and absolute loyalty to Vegeta's command made him a memorable early antagonist — less cunning than his prince but terrifying in his brute-force application of Saiyan supremacy. His presence established the enormous power gap between Earth's warriors and the galactic-scale threats they would face throughout Dragon Ball Z. In non-canon material and video games, Nappa has enjoyed comedic afterlife appearances, often depicted as a movie producer or a ghost lamenting Vegeta's betrayal.

characters.name: Bardock
characters.desc: Bardock is the biological father of Goku (Kakarot) and a pivotal figure whose actions have resonated across Dragon Ball canon in increasingly significant ways. A low-class Saiyan warrior who distinguished himself through sheer grit and battlefield experience, Bardock was unusual among Saiyans for actually caring about his son and his wife Gine, and for possessing the capacity for introspection. In the original Dragon Ball Z TV special Bardock: The Father of Goku, he led a squad of Saiyan warriors on planetary conquests until an encounter with a psychic alien granted him visions of Frieza's impending annihilation of Planet Vegeta. Ignoring the warnings of his fellow Saiyans, Bardock launched a desperate one-man assault on Frieza's orbiting ship, fighting through hundreds of soldiers to confront the tyrant directly. His final stand, defiantly hurling a massive energy blast at Frieza's supernova attack as Planet Vegeta exploded behind him, is one of the franchise's most iconic scenes. In Dragon Ball Super: Broly, Bardock's role was significantly reinterpreted: this version deduced Frieza's genocidal intentions through logic rather than visions and sent Goku away to Earth not just for survival, but because he and Gine genuinely loved their son. This compassionate Bardock softened the cold Saiyan archetype, revealing a father whose final act was ensuring his son would live free. Bardock's legacy reverberates through Goku's instinctive fighting style and his genetic potential, which Bardock's battlefield experience helped unlock before Goku's birth. His defiant spirit, meeting death head-on with a grin, and refusing to submit to fate, is the Saiyan trait most obviously inherited by his son. Few characters with such limited screen time have cast such a long shadow over the entire franchise.

characters.name: Captain Ginyu
characters.desc: Captain Ginyu is the flamboyant and dangerously powerful leader of the Ginyu Force, the elite mercenary squad summoned by Frieza to retrieve the Namekian Dragon Balls. Ginyu's combat power is formidable in its own right, capable of pressuring a power-level-90,000 Goku, but his true threat lies in his unique Body Change technique, which allows him to swap bodies with any target and permanently steal their physical form and power. Ginyu's obsession with strength and perfection drives his bizarre code of honor: he forced his squad to practice elaborate synchronized poses despite their deadly profession, and he personally evaluates new bodies by their aesthetic appeal as much as their power. On Namek, he cleverly injured himself before switching with Goku, leaving the Saiyan trapped in Ginyu's bleeding, broken body while Ginyu initially struggled to use Goku's form effectively. His plan was foiled by Vegeta, Krillin, and Gohan, forcing Ginyu to attempt switching with Vegeta and later Piccolo. Ginyu's final fate in the canon timeline was grim and ironic: after a failed attempt to steal Vegeta's body, his consciousness was inadvertently transferred into a tiny Namekian frog, in which form he spent years until being killed during Kid Buu's planetary destruction. In Dragon Ball Super, Ginyu made a surprising return when the frog-form Ginyu was picked up by a Frieza Force soldier, allowing him to body-swap into the much stronger Tagoma briefly before being permanently destroyed by Vegeta. Ginyu's combination of terrifying ability, theatrical flamboyance, and genuine tactical intelligence made him a standout antagonist — the lone Ginyu Force member who was dangerous not because of overwhelming power alone, but because his technique rendered any opponent's strength potentially his own.

characters.name: Recoome
characters.desc: Recoome is the hulking, sadistic powerhouse of the Ginyu Force whose childlike enthusiasm for violence and remarkable durability made him a standout member of Frieza's elite squad. Towering over most opponents with his massive frame and flamboyant orange pompadour hairstyle, Recoome treated battle as a playground game, laughing uproariously while brutalizing his opponents with devastating wrestling-inspired attacks. His most infamous technique, the Recoome Eraser Gun, fired a massive energy blast from his mouth after a theatrical build-up. On Planet Namek, Recoome single-handedly dismantled Vegeta, Krillin, and Gohan in sequence, shrugging off their strongest attacks with almost comedic ease — Krillin's full-power Kamehameha barely singed his armor, and Vegeta's most desperate offense only served to amuse him. His signature Recoome Kick, delivered with a joyful shout and a gymnastic pose, broke Gohan's neck and nearly killed the young half-Saiyan. Recoome's reign of terror ended with the arrival of Goku, who had completed his gravity training en route to Namek. In one of Dragon Ball Z's most iconic moments, Goku defeated Recoome with a single elbow strike to the stomach that left the Ginyu Force giant unconscious on the ground, stunning his remaining squad members into disbelief. Vegeta, ever the pragmatist, executed the unconscious Recoome with a point-blank energy blast. Though his time in the spotlight was brief, Recoome's combination of overwhelming power, theatrical personality, and unforgettable design secured his place in Dragon Ball lore. His name, like all Ginyu Force members, is a pun on a dairy product: Recoome, from cream.

characters.name: Burter
characters.role: Ginyu Force Speedster / Fastest in the Universe
characters.desc: Burter is the towering blue speedster of the Ginyu Force who claimed the title of fastest being in the universe, a boast that, while unverifiable, was supported by his genuinely astonishing velocity in combat. Electrically charged with crackling blue energy, Burter's fighting style relied on overwhelming opponents with attacks so rapid that most could not perceive them, let alone defend against them. His signature Blue Hurricane technique spun him at incredible rotational speeds, creating a devastating tornado-like vortex that trapped and battered his enemies. On Namek, after Recoome's defeat, Burter faced Goku alongside Jeice in a two-on-one battle. Despite their combined assault, Goku's vastly superior speed and power rendered Burter's trademark velocity meaningless: the Saiyan evaded everything they threw at him with such speed that he appeared to vanish between their attacks. Burter's disbelief — the psychological crumbling of a warrior whose entire identity was built around being the fastest — was as complete as his physical defeat. After being knocked unconscious by Goku, Burter met his end at Vegeta's hands, killed by a knee to the neck stomp while lying helpless on the ground. Burter's design, with his blue skin, red eyes, and elongated, serpentine frame, made him visually the flashiest member of the Ginyu Force. Like his squadmates, he took great pride in the team's elaborate synchronized poses, treating them with as much seriousness as the actual combat. His name follows the Ginyu Force's dairy pun theme, derived from butter. Burter's brief but impactful appearance encapsulated the Ginyu Force's unique blend of absurd theatricality and genuine, terrifying combat prowess.

characters.name: Jeice
characters.desc: Jeice is the flamboyant red-skinned tactician of the Ginyu Force, a self-proclaimed master of strategic combat who served as Captain Ginyu's most trusted lieutenant. Sporting flowing white hair and an Australian-inspired accent in the English dub, Jeice presented himself as the stylish, calculating member of the squad whose Crusher Ball technique — a compact, volleyball-sized sphere of crackling red energy that he could direct mid-flight — was both powerful and visually distinctive. On Namek, after Goku dismantled Recoome with a single elbow strike, Jeice and Burter launched a coordinated two-on-one assault against the Saiyan. Their combined speed and power, which would have overwhelmed any previous opponent, proved utterly useless against Goku's post-gravity-training capabilities. Jeice's strategic mind quickly assessed the hopelessness of the situation, and in a moment of pragmatic cowardice, he retreated to summon Captain Ginyu. It was Jeice who witnessed Ginyu's humiliation firsthand as the captain accidentally trapped himself in a Namekian frog's body and was beaten senseless by Vegeta while trapped in Goku's weakened form. Jeice's own end came when he attempted to flee rather than fight Vegeta, who effortlessly intercepted him and executed the fleeing Ginyu Force member with a single energy blast. Jeice's name follows the squad's dairy theme, derived from cheese. Though less individually powerful than Recoome or Burter, Jeice's tactical role and his front-row seat to Ginyu's body-swap disaster made him a narratively significant member of the squad. His combination of arrogant self-regard and prudent cowardice contrasted well with Recoome's brute force and Burter's speed-obsession, rounding out the Ginyu Force's dysfunctional family dynamic.

characters.name: Guldo
characters.desc: Guldo is the diminutive, four-eyed psychic of the Ginyu Force, the physically weakest member whose time-stopping abilities and telekinesis made him far more dangerous than his puny power level would suggest. A squat, frog-like creature with a perpetually sour expression, Guldo compensated for his physical frailty with psychic powers that violated the fundamental rules of combat. His most devastating ability, Time Freeze, allowed him to stop time itself for as long as he could hold his breath, during which he could reposition, retrieve weapons like the sharpened tree trunk he favored, or line up attacks against frozen opponents. His telekinesis was strong enough to immobilize Krillin and Gohan simultaneously, lifting them into the air helplessly while he prepared to impale them. On Namek, Guldo faced Krillin and Gohan in a match that should have been his to lose: his time-stop and telekinetic combination was nearly unbeatable until Vegeta intervened at the critical moment, decapitating the psychic with a single energy blast while Guldo was distracted with his other victims. Guldo's death triggered the Ginyu Force's vengeful escalation, with Recoome quickly stepping in to avenge his fallen comrade by brutalizing Krillin and Gohan. Despite being the weakest member of the squad with a power level a fraction of his teammates', Guldo's unique abilities made him arguably the most dangerous Ginyu Force member against unprepared opponents. His design, with four bulging eyes that moved independently, squat green body, and perpetually scowling expression, remains one of Toriyama's most distinctive alien creations. His name follows the dairy theme of the Ginyu Force, derived from yogurt.

characters.name: Zarbon
characters.desc: Zarbon is Frieza's sophisticated and ruthlessly loyal top advisor, a strikingly handsome alien nobleman who served as the tyrant's most trusted administrator and enforcer on Planet Namek. Unlike the brutish Dodoria, Zarbon cultivated an image of refined elegance, with emerald-green hair, polished armor, and a carefully maintained appearance. Yet beneath the beauty lay a monstrous secret: Zarbon possessed the ability to transform into a hideous reptilian beast whose power dwarfed his elegant base form. He abhorred this form, considering it grotesque and shameful, and only resorted to it when pushed to the absolute limit. This internal conflict between vanity and necessity defined Zarbon's combat style. On Namek, Zarbon first defeated Vegeta with relative ease, the Saiyan's 24,000 power level proving insufficient against Zarbon's base power of 23,000 plus the experience gap. However, when Vegeta returned after a near-death Zenkai boost that pushed him to 30,000, Zarbon was forced to transform into his hated reptilian form. The monster form's raw power allowed him to crush Vegeta a second time in a brutal display. Zarbon's fatal miscalculation came when he failed to verify Vegeta's death, allowing the Saiyan to recover and gain yet another near-death power boost. In their third and final encounter, Vegeta killed Zarbon with a single punch and an energy blast, the transformed advisor's final screams echoing across Namek's green skies. Zarbon's character, with his elegant facade hiding monstrous capability, and his genuine shame about his transformative power, added psychological depth to what could have been a simple henchman role. His dynamic with Frieza also hinted at the broader political structure of the Planet Trade Organization, where even the highest advisors lived in constant fear of their emperor.
characters.power_level: Base: 23,000; Transformed Monster Form: 30,000-33,000 (estimated). Killed by Vegeta after his third Zenkai boost.

characters.name: Dodoria
characters.desc: Dodoria is Frieza's hulking, pink-skinned enforcer whose brute-force approach to problem-solving made him the blunt instrument of the Planet Trade Organization. Unlike the refined and calculating Zarbon, Dodoria preferred direct, savage solutions: crushing skulls, rending bodies, and terrorizing subordinates into submission. His massive frame and jagged, spike-studded forearms were weapons in themselves, and his gruff, impatient personality left no room for negotiation or subtlety. On Planet Namek, Dodoria served as Frieza's primary muscle during the hunt for the Dragon Balls, slaughtering Namekian villagers and torturing them for information with casual cruelty. It was Dodoria who, after being cornered by a vengeful Vegeta, revealed the truth that had haunted the Saiyan prince his entire life: Frieza, not a meteor, had destroyed Planet Vegeta and exterminated the Saiyan race. This revelation, blurted out in a desperate attempt to bargain for his life, transformed Vegeta's understanding of his own past and redirected his entire character arc. Dodoria's confession did not save him: Vegeta, enraged beyond measure, blasted through the enforcer's attempted escape with overwhelming power, executing him without mercy. Despite his brutal nature, Dodoria's death was a turning point in the Namek Saga, marking Vegeta's full break from Frieza's empire and his emergence as a wildcard player in the three-way Dragon Ball hunt. Dodoria's combination of terrifying strength, sadistic personality, and his unwitting role as the revealer of Frieza's greatest secret made him a memorable early Frieza Force antagonist whose significance extended beyond his combat power. His pink, spiked, ursine design remains one of Toriyama's most recognizable henchman creations.
